001// Copyright (c) Choreo contributors
002
003package choreo.auto;
004
005import edu.wpi.first.wpilibj.DriverStation;
006import edu.wpi.first.wpilibj.event.EventLoop;
007import edu.wpi.first.wpilibj2.command.Command;
008import edu.wpi.first.wpilibj2.command.CommandScheduler;
009import edu.wpi.first.wpilibj2.command.Commands;
010import edu.wpi.first.wpilibj2.command.button.Trigger;
011import java.util.function.BooleanSupplier;
012
013/**
014 * An object that represents an autonomous routine.
015 *
016 * <p>This loop is used to handle autonomous trigger logic and schedule commands. This loop should
017 * **not** be shared across multiple autonomous routines.
018 *
019 * @see AutoFactory#newRoutine Creating a routine from a AutoFactory
020 */
021public class AutoRoutine {
022  /** The underlying {@link EventLoop} that triggers are bound to and polled */
023  protected final EventLoop loop;
024
025  /** The name of the auto routine this loop is associated with */
026  protected final String name;
027
028  /** A boolean utilized in {@link #running()} to resolve trueness */
029  protected boolean isActive = false;
030
031  /** A boolean that is true when the loop is killed */
032  protected boolean isKilled = false;
033
034  /** The amount of times the routine has been polled */
035  protected int pollCount = 0;
036
037  /** Returns true if the alliance is known or is irrelevant (i.e. flipping is not being done) */
038  protected BooleanSupplier allianceKnownOrIgnored = () -> true;
039
040  /**
041   * Creates a new loop with a specific name
042   *
043   * @param name The name of the loop
044   * @see AutoFactory#newRoutine Creating a loop from a AutoFactory
045   */
046  public AutoRoutine(String name) {
047    this.loop = new EventLoop();
048    this.name = name;
049  }
050
051  /**
052   * Creates a new loop with a specific name and a custom alliance supplier.
053   *
054   * @param name The name of the loop
055   * @param allianceKnownOrIgnored Returns true if the alliance is known or is irrelevant (i.e.
056   *     flipping is not being done).
057   * @see AutoFactory#newRoutine Creating a loop from a AutoFactory
058   */
059  public AutoRoutine(String name, BooleanSupplier allianceKnownOrIgnored) {
060    this.loop = new EventLoop();
061    this.name = name;
062    this.allianceKnownOrIgnored = allianceKnownOrIgnored;
063  }
064
065  /**
066   * A constructor to be used when inheriting this class to instantiate a custom inner loop
067   *
068   * @param name The name of the loop
069   * @param loop The inner {@link EventLoop}
070   */
071  protected AutoRoutine(String name, EventLoop loop) {
072    this.loop = loop;
073    this.name = name;
074  }
075
076  /**
077   * Returns a {@link Trigger} that is true while this autonomous routine is being polled.
078   *
079   * <p>Using a {@link Trigger#onFalse(Command)} will do nothing as when this is false the routine
080   * is not being polled anymore.
081   *
082   * @return A {@link Trigger} that is true while this autonomous routine is being polled.
083   */
084  public Trigger running() {
085    return new Trigger(loop, () -> isActive && DriverStation.isAutonomousEnabled());
086  }
087
088  /** Polls the routine. Should be called in the autonomous periodic method. */
089  public void poll() {
090    if (!DriverStation.isAutonomousEnabled()
091        || !allianceKnownOrIgnored.getAsBoolean()
092        || isKilled) {
093      isActive = false;
094      return;
095    }
096    pollCount++;
097    loop.poll();
098    isActive = true;
099  }
100
101  /**
102   * Gets the event loop that this routine is using.
103   *
104   * @return The event loop that this routine is using.
105   */
106  public EventLoop loop() {
107    return loop;
108  }
109
110  /**
111   * Gets the poll count of the routine.
112   *
113   * @return The poll count of the routine.
114   */
115  int pollCount() {
116    return pollCount;
117  }
118
119  /**
120   * Resets the routine. This can either be called on auto init or auto end to reset the routine
121   * incase you run it again. If this is called on a routine that doesn't need to be reset it will
122   * do nothing.
123   */
124  public void reset() {
125    pollCount = 0;
126    isActive = false;
127  }
128
129  /** Kills the loop and prevents it from running again. */
130  public void kill() {
131    CommandScheduler.getInstance().cancelAll();
132    if (isKilled) {
133      return;
134    }
135    reset();
136    DriverStation.reportWarning("Killed An Auto Loop", true);
137    isKilled = true;
138  }
139
140  /**
141   * Creates a command that will poll this event loop and reset it when it is cancelled.
142   *
143   * <p>The command will end instantly and kill the routine if the alliance supplier returns an
144   * empty optional when the command is scheduled.
145   *
146   * @return A command that will poll this event loop and reset it when it is cancelled.
147   * @see #cmd(BooleanSupplier) A version of this method that takes a condition to finish the loop.
148   */
149  public Command cmd() {
150    return cmd(() -> false);
151  }
152
153  /**
154   * Creates a command that will poll this event loop and reset it when it is finished or canceled.
155   *
156   * <p>The command will end instantly and kill the routine if the alliance supplier returns an
157   * empty optional when the command is scheduled.
158   *
159   * @param finishCondition A condition that will finish the loop when it is true.
160   * @return A command that will poll this event loop and reset it when it is finished or canceled.
161   * @see #cmd() A version of this method that doesn't take a condition and never finishes except if
162   *     the alliance supplier returns an empty optional when scheduled.
163   */
164  public Command cmd(BooleanSupplier finishCondition) {
165    return Commands.either(
166        Commands.run(this::poll)
167            .finallyDo(this::reset)
168            .until(() -> !DriverStation.isAutonomousEnabled() || finishCondition.getAsBoolean())
169            .withName(name),
170        Commands.runOnce(
171            () -> {
172              DriverStation.reportWarning(
173                  "[Choreo] Alliance not known when starting routine", false);
174              kill();
175            }),
176        allianceKnownOrIgnored);
177  }
178}
